name: Duke Energy — McGuire Nuclear Station
risk_note: Nuclear plant with a 10-mile emergency planning zone. In a wildfire scenario, smoke, road closures, and access disruption are primary concerns for nearby residents.

name: Duke Energy — Catawba Nuclear Station
risk_note: Two-unit nuclear plant on Lake Wylie. Serves as a regional power hub — disruption affects the Carolinas grid. 10-mile EPZ covers parts of NC and SC.

name: Duke Energy — Oconee Nuclear Station
risk_note: Largest nuclear plant in the Southeast by capacity. 10-mile emergency planning zone includes parts of Oconee and Pickens counties.

name: Brunswick Nuclear Plant
risk_note: Coastal nuclear plant. Wildfire and hurricane risk overlap. 10-mile EPZ covers parts of Brunswick and Columbus counties.

name: Shearon Harris Nuclear Plant
risk_note: Single-unit plant southwest of Raleigh. 10-mile EPZ covers rural Wake, Chatham, and Harnett counties — areas at elevated wildfire risk.

name: Vogtle Electric Generating Plant
risk_note: Recently expanded to 4 units. 10-mile EPZ covers Burke and surrounding counties in eastern Georgia.

name: Sequoyah Nuclear Plant
risk_note: TVA-operated plant on Chickamauga Lake. 10-mile EPZ includes parts of Hamilton and Bradley counties in Tennessee.

name: Watts Bar Nuclear Plant
risk_note: TVA facility on the Tennessee River. Surrounding Rhea and Meigs counties have elevated wildfire risk in drought conditions.

name: Surry Power Station
risk_note: Dominion Energy plant on the James River. 10-mile EPZ covers rural Surry County — limited evacuation route capacity.

name: North Anna Power Station
risk_note: Two-unit plant near Lake Anna. 10-mile EPZ spans Louisa, Spotsylvania, and Orange counties — rural roads are primary evacuation routes.

name: Chemours — Fayetteville Works
risk_note: Chemical manufacturing facility. Produces fluorochemicals. Fire near this site may release toxic smoke. Shelter-in-place protocols may be triggered.

name: Nucor Steel — Charlotte
risk_note: Electric arc furnace steel plant. Large fuel and chemical storage on-site. Fire risk includes stored oils and industrial gases.

name: Colonial Pipeline — Shelby Terminal
risk_note: Major petroleum pipeline terminal. Wildfire near fuel storage tanks creates extreme explosion and toxic smoke risk. Large evacuation zone may be needed.

name: Bayer CropScience — Institute Plant
risk_note: Site of a 1985 methyl isocyanate release (same chemical as Bhopal). Chemical fire here could require large-scale evacuation of the Kanawha Valley.

name: DuPont — Richmond Works
risk_note: Large chemical manufacturing campus near Richmond. Multiple hazardous materials stored on-site. Fire could release toxic compounds.

name: Eastman Chemical — Kingsport
risk_note: One of the largest chemical plants in the US. Produces acetyl chemicals and plastics. Fire could release acetic acid and other hazardous vapors.

name: BASF — Freeport Complex
risk_note: Major petrochemical complex on the Gulf Coast. Produces a wide range of industrial chemicals. Fire risk is elevated during dry Gulf Coast conditions.

name: ExxonMobil — Baytown Refinery
risk_note: Largest refinery in the US. A fire could trigger massive explosions and release of benzene, hydrogen sulfide, and other toxic chemicals. Multi-mile exclusion zone possible.

name: GreenField Environmental — Columbia
risk_note: Hazardous waste treatment and disposal facility. Fire here could release mixed industrial toxins. Nearby residents should follow shelter-in-place guidance.

name: Arkema — Crosby Plant
risk_note: Organic peroxide manufacturer. This site exploded during Hurricane Harvey (2017) when refrigeration failed. Fire/heat can trigger spontaneous explosion of stored chemicals.

name: Dominion Energy — Cove Point LNG
risk_note: Major LNG export terminal on the Chesapeake Bay. LNG fires burn extremely hot and resist conventional suppression. Large exclusion zone required.

name: Piedmont Natural Gas — Charlotte
risk_note: Urban natural gas storage and distribution hub. Fire or rupture could disrupt gas supply to much of the Charlotte region.

name: Duke Energy — Marshall Steam Station
risk_note: Coal ash impoundments on Lake Norman. Wildfire in the area can compromise containment structures and risk environmental contamination of the lake.

name: Progress Energy — Mayo Plant
risk_note: Coal-fired plant with large fuel storage. Wildfire risk in surrounding Person County is elevated during drought conditions.

name: Spectra Energy — Pine Needle LNG
risk_note: Underground LNG storage facility. Provides peak-day natural gas supply for the Piedmont Triad region. Disruption affects millions of customers.

name: TC Energy — Buckingham Compressor Station
risk_note: Transcontinental Gas Pipe Line compressor station. Serves as a key node in the eastern US natural gas network. Fire or damage affects regional energy supply.

name: Sabine Pass LNG Terminal
risk_note: Largest LNG export facility in the US. LNG pool fires can reach 1,800°F and are nearly impossible to extinguish — they must burn out. Exclusion zone is measured in miles.

name: Freeport LNG Terminal
risk_note: Major LNG export terminal on Quintana Island. Explosion in 2022 caused 3-month shutdown. Fire risk is high in dry coastal conditions.

name: Dominion Energy — Surry Substation
risk_note: High-voltage transmission substation adjacent to Surry Nuclear Plant. Wildfire damage could knock out power to the nuclear facility and surrounding region.

name: Southern Company — Plant Vogtle Energy Complex
risk_note: Combined nuclear and gas generating complex. One of the most significant power generation sites in the Southeast — serves millions of Georgia customers.
